steps.description: Audit and repair the canonical API database package layout so the implementation is truly centered under api/db/. Confirm and update package/build configuration to compile and ship the canonical db entrypoints and SQL assets, and identify any supporting canonical helpers that seed.ts/client.ts depend on.

Rewrite the canonical schema sources under api/db so schema.sql and migrations/001_initial_schema.sql are complete, internally consistent, and aligned with the requested Ship document model. Fix omissions and drift found in the previous attempt, including missing required tables in schema.sql, duplicate/legacy table definitions, correct document visibility and per-workspace ticket numbering behavior, circular-parent prevention trigger, pg_trgm support, and comprehensive indexes/partials.

Bring the canonical migration/bootstrap implementation in api/db into sync with the revised canonical schema. Ensure migrate.ts applies SQL files from api/db/migrations in order, bootstrap logic invokes the canonical schema/migration flow correctly, and naming/exports are consistent so both CLI scripts and runtime callers use the real implementation under api/db.

Repair the canonical pooled PostgreSQL client under api/db/client.ts so it satisfies the operational requirements without introducing new TypeScript/runtime errors: connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, SSL handling, env loading, and graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown with safe singleton behavior.

Rewrite the canonical seed implementation under api/db/seed.ts to create the requested demo workspace, bcrypt-hashed admin user, memberships, and representative sample documents/associations using the canonical schema only. Preserve or repair any shared canonical seed helpers needed by setup flows, and remove assumptions that conflict with the revised schema.

Convert legacy api/src/db files into thin wrappers or re-exports only, replacing their full contents after the canonical api/db implementation is fixed. This keeps existing runtime imports working while enforcing api/db as the canonical deliverable path.

Update direct callers that already import canonical db helpers so they reference the stabilized canonical bootstrap API and do not rely on mismatched legacy export names. This specifically includes scripts outside src that currently target api/db directly.

tool_input.command: cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260327c && sed -n '1,220p' api/db/schema.sql && printf '\n---MIGRATION---\n' && sed -n '1,260p' api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260327c && git diff -- api/src/db/client.ts api/src/db/migrate.ts api/src/db/seed.ts api/src/db/bootstrap.ts api/src/db/schema.sql api/src/db/ssl.ts api/src/db/welcomeDocument.ts

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260327c && python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for p in [Path('api/db/schema.sql'), Path('api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql')]:
    txt = p.read_text()
    print(p)

messages.content:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.

## Summary
Outcome: FAILED
Instruction: Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.
